title: Scanning Electron Microscopy (SEM) — secondary-electron surface imaging
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence template (genesis fan-out artifact). Physics below is grounded in canonical electron-microscopy literature for this principle.

E.description: Scanning Electron Microscopy (SEM) — secondary-electron surface imaging: electron beam scanning produces the measurement through a 4-node primitive DAG L.excitation.electron_beam -> S.scan.raster -> D.secondary_electron -> int.temporal, with time-integrated exposure and photon-shot-noise-limited (Poisson counting). Recovery is posed as a linear inverse problem that inverts the forward operator to estimate the scene-side 2D intensity. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~8; calibration-level mismatch (beam_drift, charging_artifact, beam_damage) sets the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form imaging equation.
E.forward_model: y(r) = [PSF_SE(r; kV, spot_size) conv SE_yield(r)] + n, SE_yield depends on topography and Z
E.world_state_x: Scene parameter (density / orientation / spectrum / complex field depending on principle)
E.observation_y: Electron detector output at declared geometry + noise

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 2D intensity is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by the declared priors. Stability is well-conditioned (kappa_eff ~= 8); beam_drift dominates the stability cliff; charging_artifact and the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Photon-shot-noise-limited (poisson counting) sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor, while mild Tikhonov or analytic inversion is sufficient at the nominal Omega point.

C.solver_class: linear-operator + analytic regularisation [Wiener-SE, BM3D-SEM] | linear-operator + deep neural prior [CARE-SEM]
C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: ||x_hat - x||_2 <= C1 * sqrt(sigma_signal^2) / sqrt(photon_count). Mismatch-induced bias adds + C2 * |beam_drift| + C3 * ||x - x_s||_1 (best s-sparse / low-rank approximation residual). Iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the MLE; epsilon_fn captures the monotone quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(H * W * log(...)) per iteration; learned variants: O(H W Z * F_theta_cost) per forward pass
